feat.name: Savage Attacker
entries: When you hit with a weapon, you can reroll any 1 or 2 on a damage die and must use the new roll, even if the new roll is a 1 or a 2.

entries.name: Broken Sword
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action |XPHB}, you can conjure a spectral blade from the hilt to attack with or dismiss it.

entries.name: Multielementalism
entries: When you hit a creature with this weapon, you can choose to deal one of the following damage types instead of the weapons normal damage: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder. You can not use the {@optfeature Pact of the Blade|XPHB} Eldritch Invocation to deal Necrotic, Psychic or Radiant damage with this weapon.

entries.name: Mutable Weapon
entries: You can use the {@book Crafting|5BB} rules to improve this weapon, even though it is a magic weapon. You can have up to 3 improvements on this weapon at a time, those improvements must be appropriate for a weapon of its type and the {@item +1 Weapon|XDMG|+1}, {@item +2 Weapon|XDMG|+2} and {@item +3 Weapon|XDMG|+3} improvements don't stack.
The current improvements on this weapon are:

items: {@item +1 Weapon|XDMG}: You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

item.name: Utak's Warhammer
entries: You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

item.name: Silencari Dagger
entries: You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

entries.name: Mind Controlling
entries: You can cast the {@spell Mind Sliver|XPHB} cantrip from this staff using your spellcasting or pact magic feature.

entries.name: Enthralling
entries: You can add {@dice 1d4} to any {@skill Persuasion|XPHB} roll you make. On a 4, you can no longer use this feature until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Parrying
entries: When you are hit with a melee attack roll while wielding this staff with two hands, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to add 2 to your AC for that attack, potentially causing the attack to miss.

item.name: Homing Compasses
entries: Homing Compasses come in pairs, with each pointing towards the other one as long as both are on the same plane of existence.

entries.name: Heavy Sand
entries: While the bag can hear you, you can make a {@skill Performance|XPHB} check (DC 10) as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to convince the bag of sand to increase its weight to 200 lb. or decrease its weight back to 2 lb..

sizeEntry.name: Size:
entries: Medium (about 5-7 feet tall)

entries.name: Fire Resistance
entries: You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Fire damage.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You have {@sense Darkvision|XPHB} with a range of 60 feet.

entries.name: Draconic Wings
entries: You have two leathery wings on your back that allow you to fly. You can use a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to gain a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} equal to twice your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} for this turn.

entries.name: Light Bearer
entries: You know the {@spell Light|XPHB} cantrip. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for it.

entries.name: Breath Weapon
entries: As an {@variantrule Action|XPHB}, you exhale Fire in either a 15-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone} or a 30-foot {@variantrule Line [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Line} that is 5 feet wide (choose the shape each time). Each creature in that area must make a Dexterity saving throw ({@dc 8} plus your Charisma modifier and {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}). On a failed save, a creature takes {@damage 1d10} Fire damage. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage. This damage increases by {@damage 1d10} when you reach character levels 5 ({@damage 2d10}), 11 ({@damage 3d10}), and 17 ({@damage 4d10}).
You can use this Breath Weapon a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Celestial Revelation
entries: When you reach character level 3, you can transform as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} using one of the options below (choose the option each time you transform). The transformation lasts for 1 minute or until you end it (no action required). Once you transform, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

Once on each of your turns before the transformation ends, you can deal extra damage to one target when you deal damage to it with an attack or a spell. The extra damage equals your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and the extra damage's type is either Fire for {@b Fire and Shadow} or Radiant for {@b Inner Radiance}.
Here are the transformation options:

items.name: Inner Radiance
entries: Searing light temporarily radiates from your eyes and mouth. For the duration, you shed {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} in a 10-foot radius and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for an additional 10 feet, and at the end of each of your turns, each creature within 10 feet of you takes Radiant damage equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}.

items.name: Fire and Shadow
entries: Your eyes briefly become pools of darkness, and fiery wings sprout from your back temporarily. Creatures other than your allies within 10 feet of you must succeed on a Charisma saving throw ({@dc 8} plus your Charisma modifier and {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}) or have the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition until the end of your next turn.

entries.name: Item Costs and Crafting Time
entries: The cost of non-magical items varies a lot, but magic items can be categorized by rarity, which gives a general idea of how much they cost and how long they take to craft.
Consumables (other then {@item Spell Scroll|XDMG|Spell Scrolls}) cost half as much and take half as long to craft as the values given in the table below.

entries.caption: Magic Item Value and Crafting Time
Spell Scroll Value and Enspelled Item Rarity

entries: To determine how many hours it takes to make a non-magical item, divide its purchase cost in GP by 5.

entries.name: Raw Materials
entries: To craft an item, you need to collect raw materials worth half the item's purchase cost.
If a magic item incorporates another item, buying or crafting that item is not included in the crafting cost.

entries.name: Requirements
entries: To craft a magic item, you need use an appropriate tool as listed in the Magic Item Tools table. You and any assistants must have proficiency in the {@skill Arcana|XPHB} skill as well as the required tool.
If a magic item allows its user to cast any spells from it, you must have all those spells prepared every day you spend crafting the item.

entries.caption: Magic Item Tools
rows: {@item Leatherworker's Tools|XPHB}, {@item Smith's Tools|XPHB}, or {@item Weaver's Tools|XPHB} depending on the kind of armor as noted in the tools' descriptions
{@item Leatherworker's Tools|XPHB}, {@item Smith's Tools|XPHB}, or {@item Woodcarver's Tools|XPHB} depending on the kind of weapon as noted in the tools' descriptions
{@item Tinker's Tools|XPHB} or the tool required to make the nonmagical item on which the magic item is based

entries: To scribe a scroll, you must have proficiency in the {@skill Arcana|XPHB} skill or with {@item Calligrapher's Supplies|XPHB} and have the spell prepared on each day of the inscription. You must also have at hand any Material components required by the spell; if the spell consumes its Material components, they are consumed only when you complete the scroll. The scroll's spell uses your spell save DC and spell attack bonus.

entries.name: Harvesting Poison
entries: Poisons come in the following four types:

items.name: Contact
items.entry: Contact poison can be smeared on an object and remains potent until it is touched or washed off. A creature that touches contact poison with exposed skin suffers its effects.

items.name: Ingested
items.entry: A creature must swallow an entire dose of ingested poison to suffer its effects. The dose can be delivered in food or a liquid. You may decide that a partial dose has a reduced effect, such as allowing {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the saving throw or dealing only half as much damage on a failed save.

items.name: Inhaled
items.entry: Poisonous powders and gases take effect when inhaled. Blowing the powder or releasing the gas subjects creatures in a 5-foot {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cube} to its effect. The resulting cloud dissipates immediately afterward. Holding one's breath is ineffective against inhaled poisons, as they affect nasal membranes, tear ducts, and other parts of the body.

items.name: Injury
items.entry: Injury poison can be applied as a Bonus Action to a weapon, a piece of ammunition, or similar object. The poison remains potent until delivered through a wound or washed off. A creature that takes Piercing or Slashing damage from an object coated with the poison is exposed to its effects.

entries: A character can attempt to harvest poison from a venomous creature that is dead or has the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition. The effort takes {@dice 1d6} minutes, after which the character makes a {@dc 20} Intelligence ({@skill Nature|XPHB}) check using a {@item Poisoner's Kit|XPHB}. On a successful check, the character harvests enough poison for a single dose. On a failed check, the character is unable to extract any poison. If the character fails the check by 5 or more, the character is subjected to the creature's poison.

In any case, the creature looses its poisonous features and can not be harvested from again. A creature that can shape shift can be harvested from once per transformation, but one transformation option cannot be harvested from more than once.

entries.name: Craftable Magic Items
entries: In theory, one you can attempt to craft any magic item. The following selections highlights some items that might be of interest to the party

entries.name: Enspelled item
entries: You can bound a spell into a non-magical item, transforming it into a magical item with 6 charges that regain {@dice 1d6} expended charges daily at dawn. While having the item equipped, the owner can take the {@action Magic|XPHB} Action to expend a charge and cast the spell from the item.
The spell uses the spell attack bonus and spell save DC you had when you crafted the item.

The rarity of the item is determined by the spell's level. Cantrips and 1st-level spells are {@b Uncommon}, 2nd and 3rd-level spells are {@b Rare}, 4th and 5th-level spells are {@b Very Rare}, and 6th, 7th and 8th-level spells are {@b Legendary}.

items.name: {@item Enspelled Armor|XDMG}
items.entry: It requires Attunement and must have a spell of the {@filter Abjuration or Illusion|spells|school=A;I} school of magic bound into it.

items.name: {@item Enspelled Weapon|XDMG}
items.entry: It requires Attunement and must have a spell of the {@filter Conjuration, Divination, Evocation, Necromancy, or Transmutation|spells|school=C;D;V;N;T} school of magic bound into it.

items.name: {@item Enspelled Staff|XDMG}
items.entry: It requires Attunement by a Spellcaster. It can have a spell of any school of magic bound into it, but if its last charge is expanded, theres a change it looses its magic.

entries.name: Improved Weapon or Armor
entries: You can enchant a non-magical item to grant it a bonus to its statistics or a special effect

entries.caption: Improved Weapon or Armor
rows: +1 to spell attack rolls and spell save DC
Advantage on Initiative rolls for all
+2 to spell attack rolls and spell save DC
Resistance to a specific damage type

entries.name: Rings and Wands
entries: You can use {@item Jeweler's Tools|XPHB} to craft {@filter Magical Rings|items|source=xdmg|type=ring} and you can use {@item Woodcarver's Tools|XPHB} to craft {@filter Magical Wands|items|source=xdmg|type=wand}.

entries.name: Potions
entries: You can use {@item Alchemist's Supplies|XPHB} or {@item Herbalism Kit|XPHB} to craft {@filter Magical Potions|items|source=xdmg|type=potion}.

If you make this extra attack immediately after an attack of the {@action Attack|XPHB} action that you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on from the {@condition Invisible|XPHB} condition, you can make the attack roll of the extra attack with {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}.

action.name: Swallow
entries: The frog swallows a Small or smaller target it is grappling. While swallowed, the target isn't {@condition Grappled|XPHB} but has the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} and {@condition Restrained|XPHB} conditions, and it has {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Total Cover} against attacks and other effects outside the frog. While swallowing the target, the frog can't use Bite, and if the frog dies, the swallowed target is no longer {@condition Restrained|XPHB} and can escape from the corpse using 5 feet of movement, exiting with the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.
At the end of the frog's next turn, the swallowed target takes 5 ({@damage 2d4}) Acid damage. If that damage doesn't kill it, the frog disgorges it, causing it to exit {@condition Prone|XPHB}.

trait.name: Aquatic Camouflage
entries: The leech has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth|XPHB}) checks made to hide in aquatic terrain.

trait.name: Blood Bags
entries: The leech has three blood bags in its body. When encountered, roll {@dice 1d3} to determine how many blood bags are filled with blood. The rest are empty.

action.name: Attach
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 4}, reach 0 ft. {@h}The leech attaches to the target. While attached, the leech doesn't attack, and moves with the target, using none of the leech's movement. The leech can detach itself using 5 feet of movement, and detaches immediately if its blood bags are filled or if the target dies.

A creature within 5 feet can take an action to detach the leech with a successful {@dc 13} Wisdom ({@skill Medicine|XPHB}) check. {@actSaveFail} The leech is still detached, but the target has the {@condition Bleeding|MonstersOfDrakkenheim} condition ({@dice 2d4}). Failure by 5 or More: The leech isn't detached and the target takes 8 ({@damage 2d4 + 3}) Piercing damage.

bonus.name: Blood Drain
entries: The leech can use this {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} only when it is attached to a creature. The attached creature loses 10 ({@dice 3d6}) {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} due to blood loss, and the blood leech fills one of its empty blood bags with some of the target's blood.

bonus.name: Blood Transfusion
entries: The leech can use this {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} only when it is attached to a creature and has at least one filled blood bag. The attached creature regains 10 {@dice 3d6} {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, and the leech empties one of its filled blood bags.

trait.name: Toxic Ichor
entries: A creature who deals piercing or slashing damage to the basilisk while within 5 feet of them takes 2 ({@damage 1d4}) poison damage.

action.name: Petrifying Eye Beams
entries: The basilisk targets one or two creatures they can see within 30 feet of them. Each target must succeed on a {@dc 12} Constitution saving throw or be {@condition restrained} as they magically begin to turn to stone. Until the end of the {@condition restrained} creature's next turn, they or an ally within 5 feet of them can use an action to cut the encroaching stone from the {@condition restrained} creature's body using a weapon that deals slashing damage. When they do, the {@condition restrained} creature takes 13 ({@damage 2d12}) slashing damage, which can't be reduced in any way, and they are no longer {@condition restrained}.

A creature {@condition restrained} in this way must repeat the saving throw at the end of their turn. On a successful save, the effect ends. On a failed save, this creature is {@condition petrified} until freed by a {@psionic cure ailment|TalPsi} power of 4th order or higher, a {@spell greater restoration} spell, or a similar supernatural effect.

action.name: Buffet
entries: The griffon beats their wings in mighty gusts of wind. Each creature not {@condition grappled} by the griffon within a 30-foot cone originating from the griffon must make a {@dc 14} Strength saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes 5 ({@damage 2d4}) bludgeoning damage, is pushed 5 feet away from the griffon, and is knocked {@condition prone}.

action.name: Piercing Cry
entries: The griffon lets out an earsplitting screech. Each enemy within 60 feet of the griffon who can hear them must succeed on a {@dc 12} Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition frightened} of the griffon for 1 minute (save ends at end of turn). If a creature's saving throw is successful or the effect ends for them, they are immune to the Piercing Cry of all griffons for the next 24 hours.

action.name: Overrun {@recharge}
entries: Spend 20+ feet of your movement and move that ammount in a straight line. You can move through the space of any smaller creature then you. This movement doesn't provoke opportunity attacks. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn't knocked {@condition Prone|XPHB}.

trait.name: Flora Flit
entries: Once per day, the lizard can use 10 feet of its movement to step magically into vibrant living flora (such as a bed of flowers, fungus, fruit or grain) within 5 feet of it and emerge from a second vibrant living flora within 60 feet of it, appearing in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of the second flora. Both florae must be Medium or larger. If the lizard takes fire damage, this trait doesn't function until the end of its next turn.

trait.name: Nature Stride
entries: Moving through nonmagical {@quickref difficult terrain||3} costs the lizard no extra movement. It can also pass through nonmagical plants without being {@condition slowed|MonstersOfDrakkenheim} by them and without taking damage from them if they have thorns, spines, or a similar hazard.

trait.name: Organic Camouflage
entries: The lizard has advantage on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks made to hide in flora.

trait.name: Amphibious
entries: The toad can breathe air and water.

trait.name: Standing Leap
entries: The toad's {@variantrule Long Jump|XPHB} is up to 20 feet and its {@variantrule High Jump|XPHB} is up to 10 feet with or without a running start.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 5}, reach 5 ft. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d6 + 3}) Piercing damage plus 5 ({@damage 2d4}) Poison damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it has the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition (escape {@dc 12}).

action.name: Swallow
entries: The toad swallows a Medium or smaller target it is grappling. While swallowed, the target isn't {@condition Grappled|XPHB} but has the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} and {@condition Restrained|XPHB} conditions, and it has {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Total Cover} against attacks and other effects outside the toad. In addition, the target takes 10 ({@damage 3d6}) Acid damage at the end of each of the toad's turns. The toad can have only one target swallowed at a time, and it can't use Bite while it has a swallowed target. If the toad dies, a swallowed creature is no longer {@condition Restrained|XPHB} and can escape from the corpse using 5 feet of movement, exiting with the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.

action.name: Tongue Lash {@recharge 5}
entries: {@atkr r} {@hit 5}, reach 20 ft. {@h}({@dice 1d4 + 2}) Bludgeoning damage plus ({@dice 2d4}) Poison damage. Toad lashes out with its toungue striking its target and attempts to steal an item they are holding or that is not fixed to their body. On a hit, the target must succeed on a DC (8+Str+Prof) Strength saving throw or one of their items is dropped in the space in front of the toad.

Packs of worgs roam harsh frontiers and cursed lands. They sometimes conspire with goblinoids and gnolls and might serve them as mounts.

entries.name: Worgs
entries: {@i Malicious Lupine Ravagers}
Sometimes mistaken at first for giant wolves, worgs are vicious hunters. These sapient predators can speak and often taunt their prey, enjoying the taste of fear in their meals.

action.name: Seize
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one Medium or smaller creature. {@h}8 ({@damage 1d6 + 5}) bludgeoning damage, and the target is {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 14}). Until this grapple ends, the isopod can't make a Seize attack against another target.

monster.name: Chimera (Soren's Wildshape)
languages: understands any one language but can't speak

trait.name: Volant
entries: When the chimera reduces a creature to 0 hit points, the chimera can move up to their speed toward an enemy they can see.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The chimera makes three Bite attacks. They can replace one attack with a Lion's Toss attack or a use of Dragon's Eruption, if available.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}12 ({@damage 2d6 + 5}) piercing damage.

action.name: Lion's Toss
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}10 ({@damage 2d4 + 5}) piercing damage, and the target must succeed on a {@dc 15} Strength saving throw or be moved up to 20 feet in any direction.

action.name: Dragon's Eruption {@recharge}
entries: The chimera spits a volcanic explosion at a point they can see within 120 feet of them. Each creature in a 10-foot-radius sphere centered on that point must make a {@dc 14} Dexterity saving throw, taking 27 ({@damage 6d8}) fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

bonus.name: Roar
entries: Each enemy who can hear the chimera and is within 60 feet of them must succeed on a {@dc 13} Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition frightened} of the chimera for 1 minute (save ends at end of turn). If a creature succeeds on a saving throw against this effect or if the effect ends for them, the creature is immune to the chimera's Roar for the next 24 hours.

reaction.name: Ram's Defiance
entries: When a creature within 30 feet of the chimera misses them with an attack, the chimera can move up to half their speed in a straight line toward the creature. If the chimera ends this movement within 5 feet of the creature, the creature must succeed on a {@dc 15} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}.

monster.name: Deathhawk (Soren's Wildshape)
languages: understands Common but can't speak it

trait.name: Corpse Sense
entries: Within a range of 5 miles, the deathhawk can sense the presence of Undead or corpses that have been dead for less than 90 days; the deathhawk knows the general direction these are in but not their exact locations. In addition, the deathhawk has advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks made to find Undead or corpses that have been dead for less than 90 days.

trait.name: Flyby
entries: The deathhawk doesn't provoke opportunity attacks when they fly out of an enemy's reach.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The deathhawk makes one Beak attack and one Wounding Talons attack.

action.name: Beak
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}12 ({@damage 3d4 + 5}) piercing damage.

action.name: Wounding Talons
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}12 ({@damage 2d6 + 5}) slashing damage. If the target is a creature who isn't a Construct or an Undead, they gain a bleeding wound that lasts until they regain at least 1 hit point. A bleeding creature loses 5 ({@damage 1d10}) hit points for each bleeding wound they have at the start of their turn. Any creature who can reach the target can use an action to stanch all the target's wounds, ending the effect.

bonus.name: Bloodcurdling Shriek {@recharge}
entries: The deathhawk shrieks, and each enemy within 300 feet of the deathhawk who can hear them must make a {@dc 14} Wisdom saving throw. Creatures who are immune to the {@condition frightened} condition automatically succeed on this saving throw. On a failed save, a creature is horrified for 1 minute (save ends at end of turn). While horrified, each time the creature makes an attack roll or a saving throw, they must roll a {@damage d4} and subtract the number rolled from the attack roll or saving throw.
If a creature's saving throw is successful or the effect ends for them, they are immune to the Bloodcurdling Shriek of all deathhawks for the next 24 hours.

monster.name: Hellhound (Soren's Wildshape)
languages: understands Common and Infernal but can't speak

trait.name: Hardened by Flame
entries: When the hound is subjected to fire damage, they take no damage. Instead, their skin darkens and hardens, and their AC increases to 18 until the end of their next turn.

trait.name: Stealthy Hunter
entries: The hound has advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Survival}) checks to track other creatures and on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks to hide from creatures who are unaware of their presence.

action.name: Hellish Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d6 + 2}) piercing damage plus 4 ({@damage 1d8}) fire damage.

action.name: Hellfire Breath {@recharge 5}
entries: The hound exhales flame in a 15-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a {@dc 14} Dexterity saving throw, taking 14 ({@damage 3d6 + 4}) fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. A creature who isn't a hellhound who fails the saving throw is lit on fire for 1 minute (save ends at end of turn), or until the target or another creature who can reach them uses an action to extinguish the flames. A creature who is on fire at the start of their turn takes 7 ({@damage 2d6}) fire damage. If a creature who is already on fire is set on fire again on a subsequent turn, the damage isn't cumulative, but the duration of the fire resets to 1 minute.

reaction.name: Tug of War
entries: When an enemy within 5 feet of the hound hits the hound with a melee attack, the hound can make a Hellish Bite attack against that enemy. If the hound's attack hits, the enemy is also {@condition grappled} (escape {@dc 12}), and if the triggering attack was a weapon attack, the weapon used in the triggering attack can't be used to make any more attacks while the target is {@condition grappled}. The grapple ends if the hound attacks a different target with Hellish Bite.

trait.name: Standing Leap
entries: The moorbounder's long jump is up to 40 feet and its high jump is up to 20 feet, with or without a running start.

trait.name: Agile movement
entries: The Moorbounder has +2 AC against opportunity attacks.

action.name: Claws
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}15 ({@damage 4d4 + 5}) slashing damage.

One of the many predators to stalk the marshlands and muddy foothills of Xhorhas, moorbounders are known as dangerous killers to those who try to avoid them on their travels—and as prized mounts for those willing to capture and train them. Though the process of domesticating a moorbounder is fraught with peril, these creatures can become favored hunting companions and powerful war mounts.

entries.name: Natural Hunters
entries: Moorbounders hunt with elongated tusks and retractable claws. With a vague resemblance to large hunting cats, their muscular bodies are built for combat, granting them incredible prowess at leaping and a running speed that makes them dominant carnivores.

action.name: Talons.
entries: _Melee Weapon Attack:_ {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. _Hit:_ 5 ({@damage 1d4 + 2}) slashing damage. If the target is a 3+ sizes smaller creature, they are  {@condition Grappled} (escape {@dc 12}). If the target is a larger creature, the bird attaches to them. While attached, the bird's Beak attacks against that target are made with advantage, and the bird can't attack another target. The target or a creature who can reach them can attempt to detach the bird as an action by making a {@dc 12} Strength (Athletics) check. On a failed check, the attached target takes {@damage 1d4 + 2} slashing damage. On a successful check, the attached target takes {@damage 1d4 + 2} slashing damage and is no longer attached. The bird can detach by spending 5 feet of their movement. The target also loses 5 feet of movement +10 for each sizelevel that its smaller then the bird, while the bird is attached.

action.name: Beak
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 4}, reach 5 ft. {@h}9 ({@damage 2d6 + 2}) Piercing damage, and (Fail {@dc 12} Constitution Save) the target has the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition until the end of its next turn.

action.name: Talons.
entries: _Melee Weapon Attack:_ {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. _Hit:_ 5 ({@damage 1d4 + 2}) slashing damage. If the target is a 3+ sizes smaller creature, they are  {@condition Grappled} (escape {@dc 12}). If the target is a larger creature, the bird attaches to them. While attached, the bird's Beak attacks against that target are made with advantage, and the bird can't attack another target. The target or a creature who can reach them can attempt to detach the bird as an action by making a {@dc 12} Strength (Athletics) check. On a failed check, the attached target takes {@damage 1d4 + 2} slashing damage. On a successful check, the attached target takes {@damage 1d4 + 2} slashing damage and is no longer attached. The bird can detach by spending 5 feet of their movement. The target also loses 5 feet of movement +10 for each sizelevel that its smaller then the bird, while the bird is attached.

entries.name: Pseudodragon
entries: {@i Fickle, Pint-Sized Dragon}

items.name: Habitat:
items.entry: {@filter Coastal|bestiary|environment=coastal}, {@filter Desert|bestiary|environment=desert}, {@filter Forest|bestiary|environment=forest}, {@filter Hill|bestiary|environment=hill}, {@filter Mountain|bestiary|environment=mountain}, {@filter Urban|bestiary|environment=urban}

entries: Pseudodragons dwell in scenic wildernesses, preferably where life is easy and prey is small and slow. There they behave like contented wyrms, creating tiny lairs amid ancient trees and rugged cliffs. They fill these lairs with shiny rocks, colorful shells, and unattended treasures that catch their attention, and they guard these hoards fiercely.
Pseudodragons grow to the size of large house cats, and most have red-brown scales. Some have scales with other hues or patterns—markings distinct from those of their larger draconic cousins.

Many magic-users attempt to befriend pseudodragons, hoping to enlist them as familiars. The creatures' intellect and resistance to magic make them excellent companions, and they're considered status symbols in some spellcasting circles.

Many pseudodragons prefer the finer things in life. These diminutive dragons might be inclined to aid those who ply them with treats. Contrariwise, mages who don't properly pamper their pseudo dragon familiars might be abandoned without warning. Roll on or choose an option from the Pseudo dragon Treats table to inspire a pseudodragon's taste in gifts.

entries.caption: Pseudodragon Treats
rows: Mementos from a lost friend or master.
Outlandish delicacies—like axe beak-egg omelets or mammoth-milk cheese.
The possessions of a sibling, rival, or master.
Shiny gifts, from gems to abalone shells.
To hear a bedtime story or favorite song.

entries: If you want to keep a pseudodragon happy, get used to thinking of yourself as its familiar.

trait.name: Hold Breath
entries: The clawfish can hold their breath for 15 minutes.

trait.name: Slippery
entries: The clawfishhas advantage on ability checks and saving throws made to avoid or end the {@condition grappled} condition on themself.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d4 + 3}) slashing damage, and if the target is a Small or larger creature, the clawfish can pull themself into the target's space and attach to the target. While attached, the clawfish moves with the target without provoking opportunity attacks, and the clawfish can only make Claw attacks against the target. A creature who can reach the clawfish can use an action to make a {@dc 12} Strength ({@skill Athletics}) or Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics}) check. On a success, the clawfish is detached and shunted into an unoccupied space of the clawfish's choice within 5 feet of the target. The clawfish also detaches if they become {@condition grappled}, {@condition incapacitated}, {@condition prone}, or {@condition restrained}, or if they use 5 feet of movement to detach.

action.name: Electrocute {@recharge 4}
entries: Each creature touching the clawfish takes ({@damage 4d6}) lightning damage. If the clawfish is immersed in water, each other creature within 10 feet of the clawfish who is touching the same body of water must succeed on a {@dc 11} Dexterity saving throw or take the same damage.

reaction.name: Zappy
entries: The Clawfish can use Electrocute as a Reaction, when someone tries to detach him. This makes it go on cooldown and he can't roll for recharge on his next turn.

trait.name: Flyby

classFeature.name: Speak with Animals
entries: You unlocked the magic of communicating with animals; you always have the {@spell Speak with Animals|XPHB} spell prepared.

classFeature.name: Primal Order
entries: You have dedicated yourself to one of the following sacred roles of your choice.

classFeature.name: Magician
entries: You know one extra cantrip from the {@filter Druid spell list|spells|class=Druid}. In addition, your personal connection to nature gives you a bonus to your Intelligence ({@skill Nature|XPHB}) checks equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum bonus of +1) and you can use Charisma ({@skill Persuasion|XPHB}) checks instead of Wisdom ({@skill Animal Handling|XPHB}) checks to interact with animals.

classFeature.name: Warden
entries: Trained for battle, you gain proficiency with {@filter Martial weapons|items|type=martial weapon} and training with {@filter Medium armor|items|type=Medium Armor}.

classFeature.name: Spellcasting
entries: You have learned to cast spells through studying the mystical forces of nature. See {@book chapter 7|XPHB|7} for the rules on spellcasting. The information below details how you use those rules with Shapeshifter spells, which appear on the {@filter Druid spell list|spells|class=Druid} later in the class's description.

entries.name: Cantrips
entries: You know two cantrips of your choice from the {@filter Druid spell list|spells|class=Druid}. {@spell Druidcraft|XPHB} and {@spell Produce Flame|XPHB} are recommended.
Whenever you gain a Shapeshifter level, you can replace one of your cantrips with another cantrip of your choice from the {@filter Druid spell list|spells|class=Druid}.

When you reach Shapeshifter levels 4 and 10, you learn another cantrip of your choice from the {@filter Druid spell list|spells|class=Druid}, as shown in the Cantrips column of the Shapeshifter Features table.

entries.name: Spell Slots
entries: The Shapeshifter Features table shows how many spell slots you have to cast your level 1+ spells. You regain all expended slots when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Prepared Spells of Level 1+
entries: You prepare the list of level 1+ spells that are available for you to cast with this feature. To start, choose two level 1 spells from the {@filter Druid spell list|spells|class=Druid}.

The number of spells on your list increases as you gain Shapeshifter levels, as shown in the Prepared Spells column of the Shapeshifter Features table. Whenever that number increases, choose additional spells from the {@filter Druid spell list|spells|class=Druid} until the number of spells on your list matches the number on the table. The chosen spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots. For example, if you're a level 5 Shapeshifter, your list of prepared spells can include six spells of levels 1 and 2 in any combination.

If another Shapeshifter feature gives you spells that you always have prepared, those spells don't count against the number of spells you can prepare with this feature, but those spells otherwise count as Shapeshifter spells for you.

entries.name: Changing Your Prepared Spells
entries: Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can change your list of prepared spells, replacing any of the spells with other Shapeshifter spells for which you have spell slots.

entries.name: Spellcasting Ability
entries: Charisma is your spellcasting ability for your Shapeshifter spells.

entries.name: Spellcasting Focus
entries: You can use a {@item Druidic Focus|XPHB} as a {@variantrule Spellcasting Focus|XPHB} for your Shapeshifter spells.

classFeature.name: Wild Companion
entries: You can summon a nature spirit that assumes an animal form to aid you. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can expend a spell slot or a use of Wild Shape to cast the {@spell Find Familiar|XPHB} spell without Material components.
When you cast the spell in this way, the familiar is Fey and disappears when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

classFeature.name: Wild Shape
entries: The power of nature allows you to assume the form of an animal. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you shape-shift into an animal form. You stay in that form for a number of hours equal to half your Shapeshifter level or until you use Wild Shape again, have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition, or die. You can also leave the form early as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.

entries.name: Number of Uses
entries: You can use Wild Shape four times. You regain two expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
You gain additional uses when you reach certain Shapeshifter levels, as shown in the Wild Shape column of the Shapeshifter Features table.

entries.name: Animal Shapes
entries: You can transform into any {@filter Beast|bestiary|type=beast|miscellaneous=!swarm} or animal-like {@filter Monstrosity|bestiary|type=monstrosity|miscellaneous=!swarm}, as long as the creature's Challenge Rating is at or below your Shapeshifter level minus 1 all divided by 2.

entries.name: Rules While Shape-Shifted
entries: While in a form, you retain your personality, memories, and ability to speak, and the following rules apply:

items.name: To Big to Fail
entries: You shape shift into a form larger than usual for the type of animal you transform into. For the purpose of this section the animal's stat block is modified in the following ways:

items: The animal's size increases by one category, it Strength score and Constitution scores each increase by 2, and its Dexterity score decreases by 2.

For calculating the animal's maximum Hit Points, you use the highest value on its {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB} and your level as the number of dice. They additionally gain 5 maximum Hit Points per CR (rounded down) of the animal's stat block. If the animal has a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB}, its maximum Hit Points are halved (rounded down).
You gain {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Strength checks and Strength saving throws. Damage you do to objects, structures and the environment is doubled.
The animal's armor class increases by an amount equal to your Charisma modifier.

items.name: Hit Points
entries: When you assume a Wild Shape form, your maximum {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} increase by the animal form's maximum Hit Points. Your current Hit Points adjust to keep the same percentage of your maximum Hit Points (rounded up). When you revert to your normal form or change to a new form, your maximum Hit Points reset or adjust to your humanoids maximum Hit Points plus the new form's maximum Hit Points respectively with your current Hit Points adjusting to keep the same percentage of your maximum Hit Points (rounded up).

items.name: Game Statistics
entries: Your game statistics are replaced by the animal's stat block, but you retain your creature type; {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB}; Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma scores; class features; languages; and feats. You use your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} for attack rolls, saving throws and skill proficiencies. You also retain your own skill proficiencies in addition to gaining the proficiencies of the animal. If a skill modifier in the animal's stat block is higher than yours, use the one in the stat block.

items.name: No Spellcasting
entries: You can't cast spells, but shape-shifting doesn't break your {@status Concentration|XPHB} or otherwise interfere with a spell you've already cast.

items.name: Objects
entries: Your ability to handle objects is determined by the form's limbs rather than your own. In addition, you choose whether your equipment falls in your space, merges into your new form, or is worn by it. Worn equipment functions as normal, but the DM decides whether it's practical for the new form to wear a piece of equipment based on the creature's size and shape. Your equipment doesn't change size or shape to match the new form, and any equipment that the new form can't wear must either fall to the ground or merge with the form. Equipment that merges with the form has no effect while you're in that form.

classFeature.name: Circle of the Moon
entries: {@i Adopt Animal Forms to Guard the Wilds}
Shapeshifters of the Circle of the Moon draw on lunar magic to transform themselves. Their order gathers under the moon to share news and perform rituals.

Changeable as the moon, a Shapeshifter of this circle might prowl as a great cat one night, soar over the treetops as an eagle the next day, and then crash through undergrowth as a bear to drive off a trespassing monster. The wild is in the Shapeshifter's blood.

classFeature.name: Defensive Wild Shape
entries: Starting at level 3, you can use your Wild Shape as a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} when you're about to take damage. Spend a use of Wild Shape to shape shift into a random animal form. Roll one die to select a CR uniformly from the whole number CRs you can transform into and then select a form from all that floor to that CR at random.

classFeature.name: Circle of the Moon Spells
entries: When you reach a Shapeshifter level specified in the Circle of the Moon Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.
In addition, you can cast the spells from this feature while you're in a Wild Shape form.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: You gain the {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB} feat or another {@5etools feat|feats.html} of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Shapeshifter levels 8, 12, and 16.

classFeature.name: Wild Resurgence
entries: If a feature requires you to spend a use of Wild Shape, you can choose to expend a spell slot instead.

classFeature.name: Improved Circle Forms
entries: While in a Wild Shape form, you gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Lunar Radiance
entries: Each of your attacks in a Wild Shape form can deal its normal damage type or Radiant damage. You make this choice each time you hit with those attacks.

entries.name: Increased Toughness
entries: You can add your Charisma modifier to your Constitution saving throws.

classFeature.name: Elemental Fury
entries: The might of the elements flows through you. You gain one of the following options of your choice.

classFeature.name: Potent Spellcasting
entries: Add your Charisma modifier to the damage you deal with any Shapeshifter cantrip.

classFeature.name: Primal Strike
entries: Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with an attack roll using a weapon or a animal form's attack in Wild Shape, you can cause the target to take an extra {@damage 1d8} Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder damage (choose when you hit).

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: You gain the {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB} Feat or another {@5etools feat|feats.html} of your choice for which you qualify.

classFeature.name: Moonlight Step
entries: You magically transport yourself, reappearing amid a burst of moonlight. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see, and you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the next attack roll you make before the end of this turn.

You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also regain uses by expending a level 2+ spell slot for each use you want to restore (no action required).

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: You gain the {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB} Feat or another {@5etools feat|feats.html} of your choice for which you qualify.

classFeature.name: Lunar Form
entries: The power of the moon suffuses you, granting you the following benefits.

entries.name: Improved Lunar Radiance
entries: Once per turn, you can deal an extra {@damage 2d10} Radiant damage to a target you hit with a Wild Shape form's attack.

entries.name: Shared Moonlight
entries: Whenever you use Moonlight Step, you can also teleport one willing creature. That creature must be within 10 feet of you, and you teleport it to an unoccupied space you can see within 10 feet of your destination space.

classFeature.name: Improved Elemental Fury
entries: The option you chose for Elemental Fury grows more powerful, as detailed below.

entries.name: Potent Spellcasting
entries: When you cast a Shapeshifter cantrip with a range of 10 feet or greater, the spell's range increases by 300 feet.

entries.name: Primal Strike
entries: The extra damage of your Primal Strike increases to {@damage 2d8}.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: You gain the {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB} Feat or another {@5etools feat|feats.html} of your choice for which you qualify.

classFeature.name: Beast Spells
entries: While using Wild Shape, you can cast spells in Beast form, except for any spell that has a Material component with a cost specified or that consumes its Material component.

classFeature.name: Epic Boon
entries: You gain an {@filter Epic Boon feat|feats|category=EB} or another {@5etools feat|feats.html} of your choice for which you qualify. {@feat Boon of Dimensional Travel|XPHB} is recommended.

classFeature.name: Archshapeshifter
entries: The vitality of nature constantly blooms within you, granting you the following benefits.

entries.name: Evergreen Wild Shape
entries: Whenever you roll {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} and have no uses of Wild Shape left, you regain one expended use of it.

entries.name: Nature Magician
entries: You can convert uses of Wild Shape into a spell slot (no action required). Choose a number of your unexpended uses of Wild Shape and convert them into a single spell slot, with each use contributing 2 spell levels. For example, if you convert two uses of Wild Shape, you produce a level 4 spell slot. Once you use this benefit, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Longevity
entries: The primal magic that you wield causes you to age more slowly. For every ten years that pass, your body ages only one year.

subclassFeature.name: Multielemental Hexblade
entries: You have made your pact with a mysterious entity from the Shadowfell—a force that manifests in sentient magic weapons carved from the stuff of shadow. The mighty sword Blackrazor is the most notable of these weapons, which have been spread across the multiverse over the ages. The shadowy force behind these weapons can offer power to warlocks who form pacts with it. Many Hexblade warlocks create weapons that emulate those formed in the Shadowfell. Others forgo such arms, content to weave the dark magic of that plane into their spellcasting.

Because the Raven Queen is known to have forged the first of these weapons, many sages speculate that she and the force are one and that the weapons, along with Hexblade warlocks, are tools she uses to manipulate events on the Material Plane to her inscrutable ends.

subclassFeature.name: Hexblade Spells
entries: When you reach a warlock level specified in the Hexblade Spells table, you gain the listed spells as warlock spells that are always prepared. These spells don't count against the number of warlock spells you can prepare.

entries.caption: Hexblade Spells
rows: {@spell shield}, {@spell wrathful smite}, {@spell blur}, {@spell branding smite}

subclassFeature.name: Hexblade's Curse
entries: Starting at 3rd level, you gain the ability to place a baleful curse on someone. As a bonus action, choose one creature you can see within 30 feet of you. The target is cursed for 1 minute. The curse ends early if the target dies, you die, or you are {@condition incapacitated}. Until the curse ends, you gain the following benefits:

items: You gain a bonus to damage rolls against the cursed target. The bonus equals your proficiency bonus.
Any attack roll you make against the cursed target is a critical hit on a roll of 19 or 20 on the {@dice d20}.
If the cursed target dies, you regain hit points equal to your warlock level + your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1 hit point).

entries: You can't use this feature again until you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Hex Warrior
entries: At 3rd level, you acquire the training necessary to effectively arm yourself for battle. You gain proficiency with medium armor, shields, and martial weapons.

subclassFeature.name: Elemental Transmutation
entries: Immediately after casting a warlock spell that deals Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder damage, you can use a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to change one of thouse damage types to a different one from that list.

subclassFeature.name: Accursed Specter
entries: Starting at 6th level, you can curse the soul of a person you slay, temporarily binding it to your service. When you slay a humanoid, you can cause its spirit to rise from its corpse as a {@creature specter}, the statistics for which are in the Monster Manual. When the specter appears, it gains temporary hit points equal to half your warlock level. Roll initiative for the specter, which has its own turns. It obeys your verbal commands, and it gains a special bonus to its attack rolls equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of +0).
The specter remains in your service until the end of your next long rest, at which point it vanishes to the afterlife.
Once you bind a specter with this feature, you can't use the feature again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Armor of Hexes
entries: At 10th level, your hex grows more powerful. If the target cursed by your Hexblade's Curse hits you with an attack roll, you can use your reaction to roll a {@dice d6}. On a 4 or higher, the attack instead misses you, regardless of its roll.

subclassFeature.name: Master of Hexes
entries: Starting at 14th level, you can spread your Hexblade's Curse from a slain creature to another creature. When the creature cursed by your Hexblade's Curse dies, you can apply the curse to a different creature you can see within 30 feet of you, provided you aren't {@condition incapacitated}. When you apply the curse in this way, you don't regain hit points from the death of the previously cursed creature.

subclassFeature.name: Silencari Assassin
entries: {@i Practice the Grim Art of Death}

An Assassin's training focuses on using stealth, poison, and disguise to eliminate foes with deadly efficiency. While some Rogues who follow this path are hired killers, spies, or bounty hunters, the capabilities of this subclass are equally useful for adventurers facing a variety of monstrous enemies.

entries.subclassFeature: Get in and Get out|Rogue|XPHB|Silencari Assassin|5BBPublic|3|5BBPublic

subclassFeature.name: Silencari Tools
entries: You gain a {@item Disguise Kit|XPHB} and a {@item Poisoner's Kit|XPHB}, and you have proficiency with them.

entries.name: Bloody Disguise
entries: You also gain 4 empty Viles of Bloody Disguise. You can use your Disguise Kit to extract the appearance and voice from a creature's blood into a Vile of Bloody Disguise. This process takes 10 minutes and is only possible within 12 hours after the creature's death.

As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can then use a this full Vile of Bloody Disguise to shape-shift and change your appearance and your voice to match the creature's. This doesn't change your game statistics and only works as long as the creature has the same basic arrangement of limbs that you have and is of a size between Medium and Small. This shape-shift doesn't change your clothing and equipment.

subclassFeature.name: Get in and Get out
entries: When you attack with a melee weapon, your Sneak Attack uses d8 damage dice instead of d6 damage dice.

subclassFeature.name: Keen Weapon Training
entries: Your weapon attacks score a critical hit on a roll of 19 or 20 on the d20.

subclassFeature.name: Patron Familiar
entries: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can expend a spell slot to cast the {@spell Find Familiar|XPHB} spell without Material components, summoning your Patron Familiar with the {@creature Daisy|5BBPublic|stat block variant} of your current wizard level.
In combat, your Patron Familiar shares your {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} count and can take all actions (including the {@action Attack|XPHB} action) as normal, but can't make opportunity attacks.

subclassFeature.name: Arcane Familiar
entries: The arcane connection between you and your Patron Familiar allows you to use them as an extension of yourself in your own spellcasting. You can cast spells as though you were in the Familiar's space, as long as they are within 100 feet of you. Your Familiar then takes the {@action Magic|XPHB} action to deliver the spell. They use your spellcasting ability modifier, spell save DC and {@status Concentration|XPHB}, but does otherwise count as the caster of the spell.
